data.content:
Here's an Android tombstone crash from a .NET MAUI app. Let me parse the key details and attempt symbolication of the Mono runtime frames.

**Crash Summary:**
- **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` (MAUI app, arm64)
- **Signal**: SIGABRT — abort message: `"not compatible"`
- **Memory hint**: UTF-16 text reads `"some intentional crash on startup."`
- **.NET Runtime frames** (#01–#07): all in `libmonosgen-2.0.so` (BuildId: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`)
- **Frame #08**: anonymous JIT-compiled managed code

Let me try to download debug symbols and symbolicate:

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# ELF debug symbol URL format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
OUTDIR="/tmp/sv-4893a92da5d642e4a4dc1a2741031191/symbols"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Try the standard symbol server paths
echo "=== Trying _.debug format ==="
curl -sS -L -o "$OUTDIR/_.debug" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg format ==="
curl -sS -L -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying libmonosgen-2.0.so format ==="
curl -sS -L -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

ls -la "$OUTDIR/" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log (not an Apple .ips file). The apple-crash-symbolication skill is specifically for Apple platform crashes (.ips files), so it won't be applicable here.

Let me analyze this crash log:

1. **Platform**: Android (arm64), running on an emulator
2. **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` — a .NET MAUI app
3. **Signal**: SIGABRT (signal 6) — the app called abort()
4. **Abort message**: "not compatible"
5. **Memory near x14**: Shows UTF-16 text "some intentional crash on startup."


6. **Runtime frames**: The Mono runtime frames (#01-#07) come from `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, with frame #08 being JIT-compiled managed code and frame #00 the actual libc abort call. The BuildId for the runtime library is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.

For Android symbolication of native .NET runtime frames, I'll need a different approach than what's used for Apple crashes.

To symbolicate these frames, I need the debug symbols matching that BuildId. Microsoft publishes symbol packages for .NET runtime libraries that can be downloaded from their symbol server. I can use the `dotnet-symbol` tool or directly query the Microsoft symbol server using the BuildId to fetch the symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so`.

Let me take a practical approach: parse the crash to identify the .NET runtime frames, then attempt to download the matching symbols.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# ELF debug symbol URL format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
OUTDIR="/tmp/sv-4893a92da5d642e4a4dc1a2741031191/symbols"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

# Try the standard symbol server paths
echo "=== Trying _.debug format ==="
curl -sS -L -o "$OUTDIR/_.debug" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg format ==="
curl -sS -L -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg" 2>&1

echo "=== Trying libmonosgen-2.0.so format ==="
curl -sS -L -o "$OUTDIR/libmonosgen-2.0.so" -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

ls -la "$OUTDIR/" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from MS symbol server

toolCalls.args: {"command": "which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -E 'binutils|llvm' 2>/dev/null | head -5", "description": "Check available symbolication tools"}

data.content: BuildId matches. Now symbolicating all frames:

arguments.command:
DBGFILE="/tmp/sv-4893a92da5d642e4a4dc1a2741031191/symbols/_.debug"

echo "=== Symbolicated .NET Runtime (Mono) Frames ==="
echo ""
for addr_info in \
  "#01:0x222098" \
  "#02:0x2241ac" \
  "#03:0x1abc80" \
  "#04:0x1abd14" \
  "#05:0x16dfa4" \
  "#06:0x16db6c" \
  "#07:0x16de04"; do
  frame=$(echo "$addr_info" | cut -d: -f1)
  addr=$(echo "$addr_info" | cut -d: -f2)
  result=$(aarch64-linux-gnu-addr2line -f -C -e "$DBGFILE" "$addr" 2>&1)
  func=$(echo "$result" | head -1)
  loc=$(echo "$result" | tail -1)
  printf "  %-4s  pc %-12s  %s\n" "$frame" "$addr" "$func"
  printf "        %s\n\n" "$loc"
done

data.content: Now let me clean up the temporary files:

Here's the full symbolicated crash analysis:

---

**Crash**: `SIGABRT` in `com.companyname.MauiTest` (arm64 Android)
**Abort message**: `"not compatible"` — with UTF-16 memory hint: *"some intentional crash on startup."*

**Symbolicated Mono runtime backtrace:**

| Frame | Address | Function | Source |
|-------|---------|----------|--------|
| #00 | `0x6ae1c` | `abort` | libc.so |
| #01 | `0x222098` | `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast` | icall.c:6244 |
| #02 | `0x2241ac` | `ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw` | icall-def.h:202 |
| #03 | `0x1abc80` | `do_icall` | interp.c:2457 |
| #04 | `0x1abd14` | `get_interp_to_native_trampoline` | interp.c:1732 |
| #05 | `0x16dfa4` | `handle_isinst` | type-checking.c |
| #06 | `0x16db6c` | `handle_isinst` | type-checking.c:506 |
| #07 | `0x16de04` | `handle_isinst` | type-checking.c:528 |
| #08 | `0x307c` | *(JIT-compiled managed code)* | anonymous mapping |

**Root cause**: The managed code (frame #08) triggered a type-compatibility check (`handle_isinst`, frames #05–#07) that led to `Environment.FailFast` being called via the Mono interpreter's internal call mechanism. The app deliberately crashed on startup with the message *"not compatible"*.
